title: Klaviyo vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Klaviyo and Drip email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which tool is best for your e-commerce business.
h1: Klaviyo vs Drip: Detailed Feature and Pricing Comparison
intro: Klaviyo and Drip are two of the most popular email marketing platforms designed specifically for e-commerce businesses. Both offer automation, segmentation, and SMS capabilities, but they differ in pricing structure, feature depth, and integration ecosystems. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your business needs and budget.

comparison_table.tool_b: No free tier; paid plans only
Deep e-commerce tracking and revenue attribution

comparison_table.tool_a: Predictive analytics, reviews management, broader integrations
comparison_table.tool_b: Visual automation builder, onsite campaigns, affiliate program

choose_a_if: You want to test the platform risk-free with a generous free tier before committing
You need advanced analytics like predictive sending and customer reviews management
You use Shopify or WooCommerce and want seamless store integration
choose_b_if: You want to start at a slightly lower price point ($39 vs $45)
You need onsite campaigns and a visual automation builder as core features
You plan to leverage an affiliate program to promote the tool

faq.a: Klaviyo has higher customer ratings (4.6 vs 4.4 on G2) and offers more advanced features like predictive analytics and reviews management. However, 'better' depends on your needs—Drip excels at revenue attribution and onsite campaigns. For most e-commerce businesses, Klaviyo's broader feature set and free tier make it the stronger overall choice.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Klaviyo or Drip?
faq.a: Drip starts at $39/month compared to Klaviyo's $45/month for paid plans. However, Klaviyo's free tier (up to 500 subscribers) makes it more affordable for startups and small businesses just starting out. Beyond the free tier, the price difference is minimal.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Klaviyo?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support subscriber list exports and imports through standard CSV formats. Most email marketing tools, including Klaviyo, allow you to import your contacts and migration guides are typically available. Plan for some reconfiguration of automations and segments during the switch.
